parsed_review.summary: CP00-504 is a clean, well-formed descriptor-only governance pack covering exactly the 10 planned RP16 units RP16.P07.M02.S07-S16 (Type And Shape Definition failure-recovery/security_audit/implementation/hermes_evidence/fixture rows). Scope, risk class (A), unit count, and deliverable/phase/micro-title distributions match exactly across the live next-pack-queue, closeout-pack-plan, review baseline, manifest, registry pack binding, CP504 requirements, contract projection (current_pack + p07_foundation_slice_descriptor + historical_pack_bindings tail), validators, and tests; the handoff chain CP00-503->CP00-504->CP00-505 with next subphase RP16.P07.M02.S17 is consistent end-to-end. The pack does NOT overclaim production readiness: manifest and command-evidence keep production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, and the sole claim (governance_core_p07_foundation_slice_descriptor_verified) is supported by the service descriptor/case-set builders, the three CP504 unit tests, the H16 deterministic Hermes evidence packet, and the passing local validation commands (all exit_code 0, plus a passing auth preflight returning valid JSON). Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are structurally enforced at every layer (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; all dispatches_*/writes_*/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt/exposes_* flags false). Claude is held read-only and explicitly non-final-approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt handling (receipt_shape ...no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence guards (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) and the required no-leak guards are present and inherited by CP504. index.js barrel re-exports the new CP504 functions so it needs no modification. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, docx) are correctly scoped out. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is the expected state at this read-only review step of the 9-step hardening sequence and is gated before commit — not a defect. No P0-P3 findings; pack is suitable to proceed to the remaining hardening/closeout steps under human final approval.
